text: Tarsal navicular bone stress injuries have the longest return-to-sport time among lower extremity sites, averaging 127 days.
Femoral neck bone stress injuries have the second longest return-to-sport time, averaging 107 days.
Posteromedial tibial shaft bone stress injuries have the shortest return-to-sport time, averaging 44 days.
Fibula bone stress injuries have a relatively short return-to-sport time, averaging 56 days.
More than 90% of athletes with bone stress injuries successfully return to sport regardless of injury location.
Bone stress injuries of the femoral neck, anterior tibial shaft, tarsal navicular, and fifth metatarsal carry the highest rates of treatment complications.
Bone stress injuries of the fibula, pubic bone, and posteromedial tibial shaft carry the lowest rates of treatment complications.
The anatomical site of a bone stress injury is prognostic for return-to-sport timelines and complication risk, supporting site-specific risk stratification in clinical management.
